methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Reditus Space is a Seed-stage space systems company founded by Stef Crum (PhD, prior NASA and Space Force orbital operations experience) and Will Sherman (CTO, Georgia Tech aerospace). The company has raised $7.1M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($7.1M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $17.75M. Peer-median triangulation is not feasible due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace filter (zero datapoints meeting criteria across three filters). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 25.8x across six public tickers, Reditus's revenue is not disclosed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $20M, with a median estimate of $18M. This range reflects application of a single quantitative method. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue disclosure (preventing multiple valuation approaches) and the limited availability of same-stage private comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector. Revenue trajectory and near-term milestones would be critical to refining this estimate.
